title: selliguea connexa
intro: Selliguea connexa, also known as the Malay water fern, is a species of fern native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Selliguea connexa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant activities, which may help protect against chronic diseases such as cancer and neurodegenerative disorders. In herbal preparations, Selliguea connexa is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
